definition: Small-cap underperformance (~30-40pp vs S&P 500 since 2023) is structural, driven primarily by leverage, credit quality, and rate sensitivity. ~40% of Russell 2000 constituents are unprofitable, median net debt/EBITDA is ~3.0-3.5x, and ~35-40% of debt is floating-rate.
evidence: Leverage differential: median net debt/EBITDA 3.0-3.5x vs 1.0-1.5x S&P 500
Correlation with real fed funds rate: -0.65 to -0.75
3/3 prior comparable divergences (1998-2000, 1989-90, 1972-73) resolved via large-cap decline not small-cap catch-up
open_questions: Whether rate cuts sufficient to close the gap given structural quality deterioration
Whether maturity wall refinancing triggers credit events or is manageable
